pages.full_text: The courtyard of the Hotel-Dieu, Beaune, is very decorative. Outside it was austere to [?deterbuglert]. The well is of elegant Gothic [?torought] - ironwork.
photo_captions: The courtyard of the Hotel-Dieu, Beaune, is very decorative. Outside it was austere to [?deterbuglert]. The well is of elegant Gothic [?torought] - ironwork.
pages.notes: Page contains two glued-in photos: (1) a colour postcard labelled 'Hospices de Beaune' showing the courtyard with its distinctive patterned tiled roof; (2) a black-and-white photo of a religious polyptych/altarpiece. The handwritten caption accompanies the postcard. Second word after 'austere to' is very difficult to read — possibly 'deter-[illegible]' or a French word. 'torought' in 'Gothic torought-ironwork' is likely 'wrought' (i.e. 'Gothic wrought-ironwork'). The right margin of this image contains the start of text that continues on the right page — it is cut off at the gutter and has not been transcribed here to avoid duplication.

pages.full_text:
We stopped at a supermarket to shop + eat lunch in the van, then drove north, nearly running out of petrol.
	The milometre has reached 66,000.
	We reached [?Guignicourt] + got the perfect spot by the river. After supper I saw an old lady walking by the river who went to sit on a bench. I sat next to her + we chatted about the de [?Nuydles].

Friday August 12th
A beautiful sunny morning. We left at 10. We drove north with cricket on the radio (we can pick it up north of [?Dijon]) As we reached SE of __St. Omer__, rain stopped play + started falling on us, too. We had lunch in the carpark + then went to look at L'Ascenseur à Bateaux des Fontinettes, a pool of water Takes boats up a canal, built in 1887. on the Canal Neufosse.
	It was built to speed things up for the boats + save water. The top caisson took in [?the] 30cm of water, along with the boat, to enable the balance to go down. Rubber-type type tubes were inflated aro[illegible]
pages.notes: Page number 21 visible in top-right corner. The left margin contains a rotated caption text 'The well is of elegant Gothic [?torought] - ironwork.' which is the caption from the left page postcard and has not been double-transcribed. Place name after 'We reached' is difficult — could be 'Guignicourt' (a commune in Aisne, France, on the Aisne river, which would fit the narrative of a river spot). 'de Nuydles' is unclear — could be a French surname or place name. 'Dijon' is a reasonable reading for the radio cricket pickup point, consistent with a route through Burgundy northward. The Canal is spelled 'Neufosse' in the diary (the standard spelling is 'Neufossé' or 'Canal de Neufossé'). 'Friday August 12th' — the year is not stated on this page; volume B covers 2003–2019. The diary mentions reaching SE of St. Omer on a Friday August 12th, which fits 2005 or 2011; given the milometre reading of 66,000 and context, a year cannot be confirmed — iso_guess given low confidence. Text appears to continue on the next page (ends mid-sentence).
